text.title: Channel Selection, Timezone, and Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Rules for matching communication need to channel, timezone-aware scheduling, confidentiality constraints, and common failure modes.

rule.statement: Match channel to communication need: urgent issues → phone or call; complex discussions → meeting; status updates → email or report; quick questions → chat; reference information → wiki; formal decisions → email with written confirmation.
rule.rationale: Using a single channel for all needs either creates noise (everything in Slack) or buries urgent items (everything in email). Explicit mapping reduces cognitive load for both sender and receiver.

rule.statement: Legal, HR, security, and salary topics are never on public Slack channels. Encode allowed channels per topic type in comms/plan.yaml.
rule.rationale: Confidential data posted on a wrong channel creates compliance exposure that cannot be undone; explicit channel-to-topic mapping prevents accidental disclosure.

rule.statement: Any global recurring meeting must either rotate its time across quarters or run two sessions. Document the rotation schedule. Encode acceptable hours per participant in the comms plan.
rule.rationale: A fixed "Tue 10:00 UTC" permanently excludes APAC time zones; the plan must make the exclusion explicit or resolve it with a second session.

rule.statement: Auto-send to executives, regulators, or external customers is forbidden. Agents prepare drafts; a human reviews and sends. Encode auto_send: false per recipient in the plan.
rule.rationale: A confabulated progress claim or tone mismatch in an auto-sent executive update creates reputational damage that is hard to recover from; the draft-and-review workflow is a non-negotiable gate.

antipattern.description: Too many meetings — death by calendar.
antipattern.reason: Beyond 10% of calendar dedicated to recurring meetings, delivery capacity shrinks faster than coordination benefit grows. Audit frequency every quarter.

antipattern.description: No agenda for a recurring meeting.
antipattern.reason: Meetings without agendas wander; participants come unprepared; decisions degrade to re-litigated debates. Require a committed agenda 24h before or auto-cancel the meeting.

antipattern.description: Status report colour set by PM narrative rather than metrics.
antipattern.reason: Optimism bias systematically delays RED status until a crisis; quantitative thresholds set before the project starts remove the subjective layer.

antipattern.description: Same communication format for every stakeholder.
antipattern.reason: A steering committee needs a 1-page decision brief; the dev team needs a ticket list; sending both groups the same status report wastes executive attention and under-informs engineers.

see-also.link: Close-out report cadence pairs with this plan
